title: Tests use code-under-test as oracle for roundtrip verification
description:
File: rust/lichen-ipv6/src/lib.rs:834-847

test_ipv6_header_roundtrip() verifies serialization/parsing by round-tripping through the same code:
```rust
let hdr = Ipv6Header::new(next_header::ICMPV6, src, dst);
let mut buf = [0u8; IPV6_HEADER_LEN];
let n = hdr.write_to(24, &mut buf).unwrap();
let parsed = Ipv6Header::from_bytes(&buf).unwrap();
assert_eq!(parsed.src, src);
```

This test only verifies internal consistency - that write_to and from_bytes are inverses. It does NOT verify:
1. The wire format is correct per RFC 8200
2. Interoperability with other IPv6 implementations

The test would pass even if both functions were wrong in complementary ways.

Per CLAUDE.md: 'Tests must have independent oracles: known test vectors from an external source, cross-validation between two independent implementations.'

Should add test vectors from:
1. Wireshark packet captures
2. RFC examples
3. Another IPv6 implementation (smoltcp, std::net)

Similar issue in test_echo_request_response (lines 851-863).
close_reason: Fixed test to use independent RFC 8200 wire-format reference vector for IPv6 header serialization/parsing oracle
